claim_text: Patient reports back pain for one week.
Back pain was first noticed after twisting left lower back.
Medications and home remedies have not provided relief for back pain.
Back pain is worse with movement to the left.
Patient has a history of diabetes mellitus.
Patient reports occasional left eye discomfort.
Patient denies chest pain.
Patient denies fever.
Patient denies shortness of breath.
Patient is taking Metformin 850 mg twice a day.
Low back tenderness is noted upon palpation.
Left lower back tenderness is noted.
Right lower back tenderness is noted.
Left lower back tenderness is worse with movement to the left.
Left lower back tenderness is aggravated by twisting movements.
Patient has musculoskeletal low back strain.
A possible lumbar disc issue is suspected based on tenderness.
Diabetes mellitus is under control.
A low back x-ray will be ordered.
Physical therapy will be ordered.
Ibuprofen 400 mg will be prescribed up to three times daily for pain management.
Flexeril 10mg will be prescribed once daily at bedtime for muscle relaxation.
Metformin will be continued as prescribed.
Patient will follow up with clinic to discuss the results of the x-ray, physical therapy, and a1c.
A follow-up appointment will be scheduled within two weeks for evaluation of low back pain and muscle relaxation.
